items.title: Ex-staff speak out about charity's $150k super debt and non-existent accounts

children.content: Former staff of charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation to the tune of $150,000. Under-18 staff also say the super accounts PAWS promised to create for them never existed.

gallery.altText: Two women sitting at a table both looking down at paperwork, with serious and upset expression.
A white van to the right, in focus that reads 'PAWS DARWIN', 'Desex, register, microchip & train'. Full sign in background.
A white woman, blonde / brown hair holding face of her big white dog, pouting to give him a kiss on the head.

avatar.alt: An Australian-Egyptian woman with dark brown shoulder-length curly hair smiling at camera, in a green patterned top.

items.title: Darwin man busted with 110,000 cigarettes, 330kg of tobacco pleads guilty

children.content: A Darwin tobacconist has pleaded guilty to tax evasion after police busted him with more than 330 kilograms of tobacco labelled as Chinese tea and 110,000 illegally imported cigarettes.

image.altText: A man in a black t shirt and sunglasses leaves court.

items.title: Motorcyclist's family urges court to jail woman who failed to give way

children.content: The family of a beloved member of the Northern Territory's motorsport community has urged a court to send a woman who failed to give way to him to jail over the fatal crash.

image.altText: Two women embrace outside court.

items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

image.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: In Barrow Creek a $5 note hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend Joanne Lees in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

articles.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed weeks before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO
A woman sitting at the bar inside a dim hotel, looking over the mementos plastered on the walls around her.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iranian targets 'threatening' Strait shipping

children.content: US Central Command says it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

articles.title: Carrying 400kg warheads, 'remote-controlled speedboats' sail into battle

children.content: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

image.altText: The Corsair drone boat appears to be turning in the sea.

articles.title: Trump beats his own record for strategic backflips on Iran

children.content: Donald Trump's actions this week have only confirmed he has run out of options and is now engaged in a simple war of wills, and calculations on who will run out of munitions first, with an enemy that he has underestimated from the start.

image.altText: A close-up of a man with blonde hair and a navy suit

articles.title: Global reserves that spared the worst of the oil shock are spent, IMF says

children.content: The International Monetary Fund says the global economy was spared the worst of the oil price shock because of large reserves. Now they are running out.

image.altText: Rows of white oil tanks are seen outdoors in front of a refinery.

gallery.altText: Black and white aerial view of explosion.
Smoke rises in Chabahar, Iran, in this screengrab taken from a social media video, released July 15, 2026.

thumbnail.altText: Black and white aerial view of explosion.
Smoke rises in Chabahar, Iran, in this screengrab taken from a social media video, released July 15, 2026.

items.title: Australian brand has a 'Trump account' after six-figure tariff refund

children.content: Businesses globally are clawing back tens of billions of dollars paid to United States customs after Donald Trump's so-called liberation day tariffs were deemed unlawful.

image.altText: a man in a warehouse smiling

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

image.altText: A hand holding a petrol pump hose, filling a car with fuel.

items.title: University leader says her sector is 'most prominent' for rising antisemitism

children.content: The chancellor of Western Sydney University says tertiary institutions have hidden behind freedom of expression to avoid taking action on hate speech.

image.altText: Jennifer Westacott

items.title: Scars of alleged Invasion Day terror attack run deep six months on

children.content: The scars left by what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" which threatened to devastate the crowd of Perth's Invasion Day rally still run deep, even nearly six months later.

image.altText: About a dozen police vehicles parked across a road to close it off.

items.title: Train drivers trapped, homes evacuated as wildfires rage across Canada's Ontario

children.content: Train operators in rural Canada were trapped by raging wildfires, as nearby communities were evacuated.

image.altText: Wildfires surrounding a train

gallery.altText: A swirling inferno seen from the inside of a freight train.

thumbnail.altText: A swirling inferno seen from the inside of a freight train.

items.title: Minister accuses Coalition of being 'juvenile' for AI office criticism

children.content: Tim Ayres has accused the Coalition of being "juvenile" for criticising the establishment of an Office of AI in the prime minister's department. Follow live.

image.altText: Tim Ayres in the foreground with Andrew Charlton standing behind him.

items.title: Disclosure scheme could have prevented intimate partner abuse, woman says

children.content: A survivor of family violence wants victims to have access to information about a person's previous offences.

image.altText: A woman stands silhouetted in front of a window.

items.title: Decisions made by 'faceless people' in NDIS abandoning those in need, family says

children.content: Experts say the National Disability Insurance Scheme is failing people like Lucy, whose one-on-one care for severe disability has been deemed "not value for money".

gallery.altText: A man and two women standing together for a photo.

thumbnail.altText: A man and two women standing together for a photo.

items.title: I thought I could use voodoo to win a TV competition, instead I won back my joy

children.content: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

image.altText: A voodoo doll of Kate.

items.title: From Mozart to the Beatles, prove you're a music genius with this quiz

children.content: From famous icons and musical terms to unusual instruments and classical influences in popular music, test your knowledge with this quiz.

image.altText: Celine Dion in a black leather jacket sings into a handheld microphone under warm yellow stage lights during a concert.

items.title: Too many cooks spoil The Odyssey's bubbling epic

children.content: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

gallery.altText: A man dressed as a solider stands in front of a crowd and ships.
Robert Pattinson is one of the side characters offered something meatier as murderous suitor Antinous.

thumbnail.altText: A man dressed as a solider stands in front of a crowd and ships.
Anne Hathaway dressed as a Greek princess on a film set
Robert Pattinson is one of the side characters offered something meatier as murderous suitor Antinous.

items.title: Gender quota debate revived in Liberals' soul-searching mission

children.content: Liberal Party HQ has admitted it is "in the political fight of our lives" as it canvasses ideas, including reviving gender quotas, to take the party forward.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit stares at the camera, with two people standing behind him

items.title: Telstra and telco regulator prepare for grilling over widespread outage

children.content: Telstra prepares for a grilling in parliament about the recent network outage that disrupted Australia, with the communications regulator also set to face tough questions.

image.altText: A woman in a blazer holds a document and looks serious.

items.title: GP clinic network hit by major cyber breach

children.content: Partnered Health, which operates more than 50 general practice and skin cancer clinics across Australia, has identified more than a dozen clinics where it fears patient information was taken.

image.altText: A white front reception desk of a medical clinic, with a white business sign, and a TV and open door in one corner.

items.title: Moira Deeming drops court bid against party after last-ditch plea to retain candidacy

children.content: Victorian MP Moira Deeming faces being disendorsed as a Liberal candidate for refusing to apologise to former party leader Matthew Guy, after accusing him of assaulting her at a community event in May.

image.altText: Moira Deeming walking with a folder in her hand.

items.title: Ancestral remains begin long journey home after 143 years at museum

children.content: The remains of members of the Rapa Nui people from Easter Island are "finally going home" after sitting in the Australian Museum's collection for over a century.

gallery.altText: Repatriation of Rapa Nui ancestral remains from Australian Museum

thumbnail.altText: Repatriation of Rapa Nui ancestral remains from Australian Museum

items.title: An alias and a dead man's name: Former ATO executive charged with fraud

children.content: Former assistant tax commissioner Nick Petroulias has been charged with multiple fraud offences, accused of obtaining financial advantage by deception and using false documents.

image.altText: A man's profiled outside a brick building.

items.title: Caring for the world's fastest falcon means preparing for bird flu

children.content: Peregrine falcons and wedge-tailed eagles could be among the first native species to contract a strain of bird flu that has recently arrived in Australia.

image.altText: woman with large peregrine falcon

items.title: Redistribution leaves Tasmanian MPs outside their electorates

children.content: Some notable politicians are among the 114,000 Tasmanians displaced by electoral boundary changes, and now have a difficult decision to make — stick with the electorate that voted them in, or move with the communities they've long been aligned with and live in.

items.title: Victim of violent robbery describes attackers as 'pure evil'

children.content: The 76-year-old victim of a violent robbery on Tasmania's east coast has described the people who attacked him as "pure evil".

image.altText: Man smiles at camera with cows in back ground

items.title: Man found dead near property fire in Sydney's west

children.content: Police investigate the suspicious death of a man who was found in front of a partially alight property in Sydney's west.

image.altText: Firefighters in high-vis uniforms stand on a road filled with police cars and fire trucks.

items.title: Employees sue Meta over claims AI systems selected workers for lay-offs

children.content: Many of the employees in the lawsuit took pregnancy or parental leave, during which time they would not have worked and thus had their measured output reduced.

image.altText: The meta symbol.

items.title: 'Very special' oval that hosts footy, weddings set for First Nations control

children.content: The transition of ownership is part of a government agreement with the ACT Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elected Body.

image.altText: Boomanulla Oval, Narrabundah

items.title: Hopes bones in underwater 'time capsules' will unlock more prehistory

children.content: Scientists who have been finding fossils in SA's underwater caves are hoping they can give a window into environmental changes.

gallery.altText: An animal bone at the bottom of sinkhole with a diver in a black wetsuit and holding a torch.

thumbnail.altText: An animal bone at the bottom of sinkhole with a diver in a black wetsuit and holding a torch.

items.title: Researchers develop laser technology to test drinks for methanol

children.content: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds every way.

image.altText: A man with glasses wearing a grey jumper poses next to a glass bottle with brown fluid in it

items.title: Broncos losing streak makes finals hopes virtually an impossible task

children.content: Brisbane must reach 13 wins to have any chance of playing finals in 2026, but even that wasn't enough for them in 2022 and may not be again this year.

image.altText: A group of Brisbane Broncos players stand in a line looking dejected after an opposition try.

items.title: Disgraced footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame

children.content: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

gallery.altText: Nicky wearing a grey coat and sunglasses.

thumbnail.altText: Nicky wearing a grey coat and sunglasses.
